pm_artifacts.title: Backend binding proposal for GTM Pipeline Q2 Review

accepted_payload.body: Verification should prove that each canonical capability returns only governed outcomes supported by the baseline: success for in-scope reads or drafts, clarification when critical scope is missing, restriction when actor scope is narrower, denial for unsafe or out-of-scope asks, and approval_required for operational previews. Tests should explicitly confirm that required inputs such as quarter, cohort_ref, account_names, reference_account, or target_ref are not silently inferred when the input contract says clarify on missing.
accepted_payload.rationale: The locked baseline repeatedly requires explicit governed outcomes and forbids silent guessing. Input-contract evidence also marks many critical fields as clarify-on-missing.
accepted_payload.title: Verify governed outcomes and required clarification behavior

accepted_payload.body: Verification should confirm that runtime metadata, regression coverage, and developer-facing evidence all preserve the canonical capability IDs and their declared input contracts without paraphrasing or alias drift. Checks should cover atomic and composed capabilities across pipeline review, enrichment, prioritization, routing preview, and outreach drafting so the same contract surface is visible from design through runtime evidence.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline makes canonical capability preservation a product requirement, and the evidence bundle already provides completed capability inventory, input contracts, runtime governance, and composition metadata.
accepted_payload.title: Verify preservation of the canonical capability contract

accepted_payload.body: Verification should include named actor-differentiated cases that show how visibility, masking, allowed scope, and governed outcomes change by actor identity. At minimum, checks should prove that a broader GTM leader can receive allowed forecast or product pipeline summaries while narrower actors are restricted from disallowed scope or receive masked financial values, and that the system can explain why the outcomes differed.
accepted_payload.rationale: Actor-aware governance is central in the baseline and is directly supported by scenarios for forecast restriction/masking and audit explanation of different outcomes.
accepted_payload.title: Verify actor-aware restriction, masking, and differential outcomes

accepted_payload.body: Verification should prove that read-oriented capabilities return bounded summaries, explainable rollups, contributors, or rationale rather than raw row-level exports, raw enrichment dumps, or raw model-feature details. Coverage should include forecast, stage bottlenecks, sales team performance, product pipeline, account enrichment, lookalike analysis, and prioritization outputs, with explicit denial or exclusion checks for unsupported export-style requests.
accepted_payload.rationale: Runtime governance evidence explicitly marks these capabilities as summary or recommendation outputs and explicitly excludes raw_data_export or raw_model_features.
accepted_payload.title: Verify bounded read contracts and non-export guardrails

accepted_payload.body: Verification should prove that reassignment planning, follow-up preparation, routing preview, and other operational-preview capabilities stop at approval_required and do not execute downstream mutations. Checks should confirm that preview content is returned, approval execution is not produced by these calls, and direct execution asks are denied or stopped rather than carried out.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline requires a hard separation between preparation and execution, and runtime governance marks these capabilities as approval_gated with approval_required outputs and no execution side effects.
accepted_payload.title: Verify hard approval boundaries for operational previews

accepted_payload.body: Verification should cover composed flows such as prioritized target to outreach draft, at-risk pipeline review to enrichment, and prioritization to routing preview. For each composed flow, checks should confirm that bounded scope is preserved across steps, child clarification or denial propagates correctly, lineage is recorded across parent and child invocations, and the flow does not blur service-owned responsibilities into a generic query-anything surface.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline explicitly requires governed cross-service composition, and the composition evidence includes propagation policy and parent-child audit lineage for composed capabilities.
accepted_payload.title: Verify cross-service composition preserves scope, lineage, and service boundaries

accepted_payload.body: Verification should prove that outreach capabilities produce draft content only, require an explicit target or bounded prioritized cohort as defined by contract, support bounded variants when requested, and deny send behavior, transcript dumps, or backend mutation. Checks should distinguish direct draft generation from composed prioritized outreach so prioritization logic does not leak into simple target-based drafting and send behavior never appears in either path.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline and runtime governance both define outreach as draft-only, with explicit denial of send-like behavior and separate contracts for direct and composed outreach flows.
accepted_payload.title: Verify outreach remains draft-only and target-bounded

accepted_payload.body: Verification should require evidence that a reviewer can reconstruct who asked, which actor identity applied, which capability ran, which normalized parameters were used, what governed outcome occurred, and why another actor may have received a different result. This should be validated across success, clarification, restriction, denial, masking, and approval_required outcomes, not only happy-path success cases.
accepted_payload.rationale: Auditability is a first-class business requirement in the baseline and is reinforced by the dedicated scenario about explaining different governed outcomes after the fact.
accepted_payload.title: Verify post-hoc audit reconstruction for single-service and composed calls

items.title: Verify actor-aware restriction, masking, and differential outcomes

items.body: Verification should prove that read-oriented capabilities return bounded summaries, explainable rollups, contributors, or rationale rather than raw row-level exports, raw enrichment dumps, or raw model-feature details. Coverage should include forecast, stage bottlenecks, sales team performance, product pipeline, account enrichment, lookalike analysis, and prioritization outputs, with explicit denial or exclusion checks for unsupported export-style requests.
items.rationale: Runtime governance evidence explicitly marks these capabilities as summary or recommendation outputs and explicitly excludes raw_data_export or raw_model_features.
items.title: Verify bounded read contracts and non-export guardrails

items.body: Verification should prove that reassignment planning, follow-up preparation, routing preview, and other operational-preview capabilities stop at approval_required and do not execute downstream mutations. Checks should confirm that preview content is returned, approval execution is not produced by these calls, and direct execution asks are denied or stopped rather than carried out.
items.rationale: The baseline requires a hard separation between preparation and execution, and runtime governance marks these capabilities as approval_gated with approval_required outputs and no execution side effects.
items.title: Verify hard approval boundaries for operational previews

items.body: Verification should cover composed flows such as prioritized target to outreach draft, at-risk pipeline review to enrichment, and prioritization to routing preview. For each composed flow, checks should confirm that bounded scope is preserved across steps, child clarification or denial propagates correctly, lineage is recorded across parent and child invocations, and the flow does not blur service-owned responsibilities into a generic query-anything surface.
items.rationale: The baseline explicitly requires governed cross-service composition, and the composition evidence includes propagation policy and parent-child audit lineage for composed capabilities.
items.title: Verify cross-service composition preserves scope, lineage, and service boundaries

items.body: Verification should prove that outreach capabilities produce draft content only, require an explicit target or bounded prioritized cohort as defined by contract, support bounded variants when requested, and deny send behavior, transcript dumps, or backend mutation. Checks should distinguish direct draft generation from composed prioritized outreach so prioritization logic does not leak into simple target-based drafting and send behavior never appears in either path.
items.rationale: The baseline and runtime governance both define outreach as draft-only, with explicit denial of send-like behavior and separate contracts for direct and composed outreach flows.
items.title: Verify outreach remains draft-only and target-bounded

items.body: Verification should require evidence that a reviewer can reconstruct who asked, which actor identity applied, which capability ran, which normalized parameters were used, what governed outcome occurred, and why another actor may have received a different result. This should be validated across success, clarification, restriction, denial, masking, and approval_required outcomes, not only happy-path success cases.
items.rationale: Auditability is a first-class business requirement in the baseline and is reinforced by the dedicated scenario about explaining different governed outcomes after the fact.
items.title: Verify post-hoc audit reconstruction for single-service and composed calls

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can request bounded read capabilities across pipeline, enrichment, prioritization, and outreach, and can request operational previews such as reassignment or follow-up preparation rather than direct execution.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: May encounter approval-gated previews for write-adjacent actions. Final approval authority is not explicit in the brief and should be confirmed rather than assumed.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: GTM leadership actor focused on pipeline health, forecast posture, bottlenecks, team performance, product performance, prioritization outputs, and outreach drafts within governed boundaries.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can review broad GTM summaries and cross-cutting business views, but still remains subject to actor-aware restriction and masking where the contract requires different visibility by actor.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can request bounded analytics, enrichment context, prioritization recommendations, routing previews, follow-up preparation, and reassignment previews within allowed scope.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Strong candidate for approval involvement on operational previews, but the brief does not explicitly assign final approval authority by action type; confirm before encoding approver status.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Revenue operations actor responsible for governed review of pipeline, forecast, bottlenecks, prioritization, and operational preparation flows.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can review broad operational summaries and preparation outputs, but still remains subject to actor-aware restriction and masking rules defined by capability.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can request bounded reads and draft/preparation behaviors within East-region scope, including pipeline review, forecast shape, product or team views, enrichment context, prioritization outputs, and outreach drafts for selected targets.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Should expect approval stops for write-adjacent preparation flows. The brief does not establish this actor as an approver and suggests lower-authority actors may be denied where higher-authority actors are allowed.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Regional account-facing actor for East-region GTM review and bounded follow-up work.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Should only see East-region or otherwise actor-visible slices. Requests for West or broader unauthorized scope should return restricted, and some financial values may be masked when shape is visible but full numbers are not.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can request bounded reads and draft/preparation behaviors within West-region scope, including pipeline review, forecast shape, product or team views, enrichment context, prioritization outputs, and outreach drafts for selected targets.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Should expect approval stops for write-adjacent preparation flows. The brief does not establish this actor as an approver and suggests lower-authority actors may be denied where higher-authority actors are allowed.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Regional account-facing actor for West-region GTM review and bounded follow-up work.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Should only see West-region or otherwise actor-visible slices. Requests for East or broader unauthorized scope should return restricted, and some financial values may be masked when shape is visible but full numbers are not.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can request bounded read-oriented capabilities such as forecast, bottleneck, team, product, enrichment, and prioritization summaries, but should stay inside declared slice and scope surfaces.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: No explicit approval authority is stated. Treat as non-approver until clarified, especially for routing, reassignment, and follow-up execution paths.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Analyst actor focused on governed read access to GTM summaries, comparisons, and explainable evidence without raw export or unconstrained query behavior.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can inspect bounded analytic outputs where allowed, but remains subject to actor-aware masking and restriction. Should not receive raw row-level exports, raw model features, raw enrichment dumps, or transcript dumps.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: sales_leader approval_required access to Operational Previews should return approval_stop. Allow bounded preparation of follow-up, reassignment, or routing previews, but stop before any downstream execution or mutation. Supported by the umbrella spec and preview-specific specs that distinguish preparation from execution.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow bounded preparation of follow-up, reassignment, or routing previews, but stop before any downstream execution or mutation.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: rev_ops_manager approval_required access to Operational Previews should return approval_stop. Allow bounded preparation of follow-up, reassignment, or routing previews, but stop before any downstream execution or mutation. Applies to reassignment preview, routing preparation, and other write-adjacent preparation flows.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow bounded preparation of follow-up, reassignment, or routing previews, but stop before any downstream execution or mutation.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: account_manager_east restricted access to Actor-Aware Governance should return masked_or_restricted_result. When a request exceeds the actor's allowed regional boundary, return a restricted result rather than exposing the broader scope. The source repeatedly requires actor-aware restriction for region requests beyond allowed boundaries.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: When a request exceeds the actor's allowed regional boundary, return a restricted result rather than exposing the broader scope.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: account_manager_west restricted access to Actor-Aware Governance should return masked_or_restricted_result. permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: When a request exceeds the actor's allowed regional boundary, return a restricted result rather than exposing the broader scope.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: sales_analyst bounded access to Actor-Aware Governance should return masked_or_restricted_result. Allow bounded analytic visibility where permitted, but mask financial values when the actor can see shape or trends without full numbers. The forecast, bottleneck, team-performance, and product-pipeline specs all require actor-aware masking for some actors.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow bounded analytic visibility where permitted, but mask financial values when the actor can see shape or trends without full numbers.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: sales_leader bounded access to Pipeline Review Analytics should return clarification_required. If a forecast, bottleneck, team-performance, or product-pipeline request is missing a critical quarter, ask for clarification instead of guessing. Applies to bounded analytics reads where quarter is a required input.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: If a forecast, bottleneck, team-performance, or product-pipeline request is missing a critical quarter, ask for clarification instead of guessing.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: rev_ops_manager bounded access to Pipeline Review Analytics should return clarification_required. If a forecast, bottleneck, team-performance, or product-pipeline request is missing a critical quarter, ask for clarification instead of guessing. Applies to bounded analytics reads where quarter is a required input.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: If a forecast, bottleneck, team-performance, or product-pipeline request is missing a critical quarter, ask for clarification instead of guessing.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: sales_leader denied access to Account Enrichment should return deny_request. Deny raw bulk enrichment export, unsupported source exposure, outreach-only requests, lead-scoring requests, and downstream mutation requests in the enrichment service. The enrichment service is bounded, read-only, and must not expose raw unconstrained enrichment exports.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Deny raw bulk enrichment export, unsupported source exposure, outreach-only requests, lead-scoring requests, and downstream mutation requests in the enrichment service.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: rev_ops_manager approval_required access to Work Prioritization and Routing should return approval_stop. Allow bounded scoring, ranking, and routing recommendation preparation, but stop at approval before route execution or CRM mutation. The prioritization service distinguishes recommendation from execution.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow bounded scoring, ranking, and routing recommendation preparation, but stop at approval before route execution or CRM mutation.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: sales_leader approval_required access to Work Prioritization and Routing should return approval_stop. Allow bounded scoring, ranking, and routing recommendation preparation, but stop at approval before route execution or CRM mutation. The prioritization service distinguishes recommendation from execution.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow bounded scoring, ranking, and routing recommendation preparation, but stop at approval before route execution or CRM mutation.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: sales_leader bounded access to Outreach Drafting should return bounded_result. Allow bounded draft generation and variants for an explicit target and purpose, but do not send messages or expose raw transcript or training-corpus content. The outreach service is draft-only in the first cut.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow bounded draft generation and variants for an explicit target and purpose, but do not send messages or expose raw transcript or training-corpus content.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: rev_ops_manager bounded access to Outreach Drafting should return bounded_result. Allow bounded draft generation and variants for an explicit target and purpose, but do not send messages or expose raw transcript or training-corpus content. The outreach service is draft-only in the first cut.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow bounded draft generation and variants for an explicit target and purpose, but do not send messages or expose raw transcript or training-corpus content.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: sales_leader bounded access to Auditability and Validation should return direct_result. Governed calls must remain reconstructable with actor identity, capability used, normalized parameters, outcome type, and explanation for actor-specific differences. This is a cross-cutting trust requirement rather than a user-facing business action.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Governed calls must remain reconstructable with actor identity, capability used, normalized parameters, outcome type, and explanation for actor-specific differences.

product_alignment.approval_posture_formalization: Read-oriented analytics, enrichment, and draft generation can return governed results directly when in scope. Operational next-step capabilities may prepare previews, but routing, reassignment, follow-up execution, and other write-adjacent actions must stop at approval_required, with authority-sensitive outcomes and durable reviewability.
product_alignment.governed_behavior_formalization: The product must answer recurring GTM questions through bounded, explainable capabilities with stable parameters and clear service ownership. It must clarify missing critical inputs instead of guessing, restrict or mask results when actor scope is limited, deny raw export or other out-of-scope requests, remain read-only or draft-only where specified, and preserve the same governed boundaries across multi-service compositions.

required_anip_support: Support service-owned cross-service capability semantics.
Preserve bounded target selection in the public contract.
Make the composed flow auditable across both services.
required_behaviors: Keep the target explicit and bounded between services.
Do not silently route leads or send messages.
Do not mix enrichment or scoring behavior into the outreach step.
Preserve clarification and denial behavior at the correct step.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Draft outreach for a prioritized target without leaking into routing or send behavior

scenario_formalizations.primary_capability: ask user to confirm which runtime or review surface should own this scenario
required_anip_support: Expose enough runtime metadata and validation surface to compare intended and observed behavior.
Preserve auditability across clarification, restriction, denial, masking, success, and approval-required outcomes.
Support review across single-service and compound flows.
required_behaviors: A reviewer can reconstruct actor, capability, normalized parameters, and governed outcome.
The system can explain why two actors received different results.
Audit is part of the trust story, not an internal-only concern.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Explain why two actors received different governed outcomes

required_anip_support: Support explicit service-owned capability IDs across the chain.
Preserve governed boundaries across multi-service execution.
Make each step auditable with actor, parameters, and outcome.
required_behaviors: Keep the account set bounded across the service chain.
Do not expose raw enrichment data.
Preserve clarification, denial, and actor-aware boundaries at each step.
Do not turn the composition into generic cross-system exploration.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Move from at-risk pipeline review into bounded account enrichment

Return explainable performance rows with pipeline and risk context.
Clarify missing quarter instead of guessing.
Restrict or mask results when actor visibility is narrower.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Review sales team performance for a quarter by one allowed slice

required_anip_support: Expose declared slice inputs and bounded output shape.
Execute aggregate bottleneck reads through the semantic layer.
Support explicit clarification, restriction, masking, and denial outcomes.
required_behaviors: Require a quarter rather than guessing.
Support one bounded slice dimension at a time.
Return top bottleneck rows with explainable evidence.
Restrict or mask results based on actor visibility.
Deny raw exports or unsupported slicing.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Review stage bottlenecks for a quarter with one declared slice

required_anip_support: Return explicit restricted and masked outcomes in the runtime contract.
Preserve actor-aware policy evaluation as reviewable behavior, not hidden prompt logic.
Record why two actors received different results.
required_behaviors: Actor identity changes what scope is visible.
Requests beyond allowed region or ownership boundary return restricted behavior.
Some actors may see forecast shape but not full financial values.
The system must not bypass restriction or masking rules.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Apply actor-aware restriction or masking on forecast visibility

required_anip_support: Represent clarification as an explicit governed outcome.
Expose required input metadata so missing quarter can be detected consistently.
Log the clarification stop for audit and regression review.
required_behaviors: Do not infer the quarter from current date or prior conversation alone.
Ask for the minimum missing field needed to continue.
Resume bounded forecast behavior only after the quarter is provided.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Clarify missing quarter before running a forecast

required_anip_support: Expose a stable forecast capability with declared input metadata for quarter, forecast mode, and optional scope.
Execute aggregate forecast reads through the semantic layer.
Return governed outcomes that can show success, clarification, restriction, or masking.
Preserve auditability of actor, normalized parameters, and outcome.
required_behaviors: Require a declared quarter rather than guessing one.
Support declared forecast modes such as risk_adjusted, likely, and best_case only.
Allow optional regional or owner-visible scoping when permitted for the actor.
Return explainable stage rollups and top contributors.
Do not expose raw row-level CRM exports.
Do not mutate downstream systems.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Review a bounded quarterly pipeline forecast with explainable rollups

service_topology_bindings.implementation_notes:
Accepted assistant guidance:
- Formalize the capability surface each service must expose: Define the stable capability contracts each service is expected to own, including which actions are read-only, which require approval, and which must stop for clarification.
  Why: The generator and verifier need stable capability ownership, not only narrative service descriptions.
- Describe the required coordination edges between services: Capture where one service depends on another for bounded context, approvals, or downstream execution so coordination can be represented explicitly instead of as hidden glue.
  Why: Coordination edges are easier to validate when they are declared before implementation.
- Keep approval boundaries outside the main happy path: Identify which service should prepare a proposed action and which surface or role must approve it before the mutation boundary is crossed.
  Why: Approval semantics should be explicit in service design rather than buried in one implementation path.
- Preserve explanation and traceability across service boundaries: Define which service boundaries must preserve explanation, lineage, or durable evidence so later verification can reconstruct why a result or stop condition occurred.
  Why: Cross-service traceability should be deliberate, not inferred after implementation exists.

non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The umbrella and service specs repeatedly require bounded capabilities instead of unconstrained CRM, warehouse, BI, or query-language behavior.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.non_goal: This showcase is not a generic "query anything in CRM or the warehouse" agent surface.

non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: Multiple source specs deny raw exports across pipeline, enrichment, prioritization, and outreach surfaces.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.non_goal: This showcase does not expose raw row-level CRM, enrichment, scoring, transcript, or training-data exports through the agent experience.

non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The source material consistently separates preparation from execution and requires approval stops before write-adjacent actions.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.non_goal: This showcase does not execute routing, reassignment, follow-up creation, outreach sending, or other downstream mutations without explicit approval.

non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: Actor-specific visibility differences are a core governed behavior across the pipeline and umbrella specs.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.non_goal: This showcase does not collapse actor-aware restriction, masking, and scope boundaries into one broad result for all users.

non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The umbrella spec explicitly preserves bounded service ownership and rejects one giant merged service or hidden prompt logic as the policy engine.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.non_goal: This showcase is not one merged GTM super-service with hidden cross-service policy logic.

non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The umbrella spec explicitly rejects hidden prompt logic acting as the policy engine and requires reviewable governed behavior.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.non_goal: This showcase does not rely on hidden prompt-only interpretation to decide governance, approvals, denials, or restrictions.

non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The source specs repeatedly state that behavior is representative, bounded, and not an exhaustive language or workflow inventory.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.non_goal: This showcase is not trying to cover every GTM workflow or every possible user wording in the first release.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Source business specs are visible in the project, derived PM and developer artifacts trace back to them, and runtime validation references the same governed capability surface.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: An informed reviewer can see that the GTM business intent was written first and that PM-facing requirements, developer design, and running service behavior derive from that source rather than hidden prompt logic.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Representative scenario review shows supported requests resolve through declared capability surfaces, while raw export, arbitrary query, and unsupported analysis requests are denied or constrained.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: The system answers recurring GTM questions through bounded capabilities with stable inputs and governed outcomes instead of generic CRM, BI, or raw export behavior.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Representative runs across pipeline, forecast, bottleneck, team, product, enrichment, prioritization, outreach, and reassignment flows return usable governed outputs with bounded explanation and no unintended mutation.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: For in-scope requests, users receive explainable summaries, rankings, previews, or drafts that match the intended capability family and stay read-only or draft-only unless an approval-gated preview is explicitly allowed.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Runtime review of ambiguous scenarios shows clarification responses for missing required fields rather than inferred defaults.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Missing critical inputs trigger explicit clarification instead of guessing, including quarter, account scope, cohort, routing target, persona, or outreach target when required by the capability.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Comparative actor review shows allowed, masked, restricted, or denied outcomes vary appropriately by actor identity and requested scope.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Actor-aware restriction and masking remain visible in runtime behavior so that different actors can receive different allowed scope, field visibility, or outcomes for the same business question.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Preview scenarios return approval-gated outputs and do not execute writes; higher- and lower-authority actors show the expected differences in preparation versus denial.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Operational preparation flows stop cleanly at approval_required before any routing, reassignment, follow-up execution, or other downstream mutation.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Cross-service scenarios show each step uses the canonical owning capability surface, with no hidden consumer-side policy substitution or merged super-service behavior.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Service ownership and capability boundaries remain explicit across the showcase, including compound flows that span more than one service.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Representative multi-step flows demonstrate that governance survives service chaining rather than disappearing once orchestration begins.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Compound workflows preserve the same governed behaviors as single-service calls, including clarification, denial, restriction, masking, and approval stops at the correct step in the chain.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Audit review for representative calls can explain both the final outcome and why different actors received different results.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Every governed call is auditable enough to reconstruct who asked, which actor identity applied, which capability ran, which normalized parameters were used, and why the outcome was success, clarification, restriction, denial, or approval-required.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Local reproduction, Studio inspection, runtime metadata validation, and regression results all confirm the implementation still matches the intended bounded design.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: The same governed services can be reproduced locally, inspected in Studio, and validated end to end through runtime checks and regression-backed review across supported flows.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can request bounded read capabilities across pipeline, enrichment, prioritization, and outreach, and can request operational previews such as reassignment or follow-up preparation rather than direct execution.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: May encounter approval-gated previews for write-adjacent actions. Final approval authority is not explicit in the brief and should be confirmed rather than assumed.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: GTM leadership actor focused on pipeline health, forecast posture, bottlenecks, team performance, product performance, prioritization outputs, and outreach drafts within governed boundaries.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can review broad GTM summaries and cross-cutting business views, but still remains subject to actor-aware restriction and masking where the contract requires different visibility by actor.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can request bounded analytics, enrichment context, prioritization recommendations, routing previews, follow-up preparation, and reassignment previews within allowed scope.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Strong candidate for approval involvement on operational previews, but the brief does not explicitly assign final approval authority by action type; confirm before encoding approver status.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Revenue operations actor responsible for governed review of pipeline, forecast, bottlenecks, prioritization, and operational preparation flows.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can review broad operational summaries and preparation outputs, but still remains subject to actor-aware restriction and masking rules defined by capability.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can request bounded reads and draft/preparation behaviors within East-region scope, including pipeline review, forecast shape, product or team views, enrichment context, prioritization outputs, and outreach drafts for selected targets.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Should expect approval stops for write-adjacent preparation flows. The brief does not establish this actor as an approver and suggests lower-authority actors may be denied where higher-authority actors are allowed.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Regional account-facing actor for East-region GTM review and bounded follow-up work.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Should only see East-region or otherwise actor-visible slices. Requests for West or broader unauthorized scope should return restricted, and some financial values may be masked when shape is visible but full numbers are not.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can request bounded reads and draft/preparation behaviors within West-region scope, including pipeline review, forecast shape, product or team views, enrichment context, prioritization outputs, and outreach drafts for selected targets.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Should expect approval stops for write-adjacent preparation flows. The brief does not establish this actor as an approver and suggests lower-authority actors may be denied where higher-authority actors are allowed.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Regional account-facing actor for West-region GTM review and bounded follow-up work.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Should only see West-region or otherwise actor-visible slices. Requests for East or broader unauthorized scope should return restricted, and some financial values may be masked when shape is visible but full numbers are not.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can request bounded read-oriented capabilities such as forecast, bottleneck, team, product, enrichment, and prioritization summaries, but should stay inside declared slice and scope surfaces.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: No explicit approval authority is stated. Treat as non-approver until clarified, especially for routing, reassignment, and follow-up execution paths.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Analyst actor focused on governed read access to GTM summaries, comparisons, and explainable evidence without raw export or unconstrained query behavior.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can inspect bounded analytic outputs where allowed, but remains subject to actor-aware masking and restriction. Should not receive raw row-level exports, raw model features, raw enrichment dumps, or transcript dumps.

permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow bounded preparation of follow-up, reassignment, or routing previews, but stop before any downstream execution or mutation.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: account_manager_east restricted access to Actor-Aware Governance should return masked_or_restricted_result. When a request exceeds the actor's allowed regional boundary, return a restricted result rather than exposing the broader scope. permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: When a request exceeds the actor's allowed regional boundary, return a restricted result rather than exposing the broader scope.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: sales_analyst bounded access to Actor-Aware Governance should return masked_or_restricted_result. Allow bounded analytic visibility where permitted, but mask financial values when the actor can see shape or trends without full numbers. The forecast, bottleneck, team-performance, and product-pipeline specs all require actor-aware masking for some actors.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow bounded analytic visibility where permitted, but mask financial values when the actor can see shape or trends without full numbers.

The enrichment service is bounded, read-only, and must not expose raw unconstrained enrichment exports.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Deny raw bulk enrichment export, unsupported source exposure, outreach-only requests, lead-scoring requests, and downstream mutation requests in the enrichment service.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: sales_leader bounded access to Outreach Drafting should return bounded_result. Allow bounded draft generation and variants for an explicit target and purpose, but do not send messages or expose raw transcript or training-corpus content. permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: sales_leader bounded access to Auditability and Validation should return direct_result. Governed calls must remain reconstructable with actor identity, capability used, normalized parameters, outcome type, and explanation for actor-specific differences. This is a cross-cutting trust requirement rather than a user-facing business action.

product_alignment.approval_posture_formalization: Read-oriented analytics, enrichment, and draft generation can return governed results directly when in scope. Operational next-step capabilities may prepare previews, but routing, reassignment, follow-up execution, and other write-adjacent actions must stop at approval_required, with authority-sensitive outcomes and durable reviewability.
product_alignment.governed_behavior_formalization: The product must answer recurring GTM questions through bounded, explainable capabilities with stable parameters and clear service ownership. It must clarify missing critical inputs instead of guessing, restrict or mask results when actor scope is limited, deny raw export or other out-of-scope requests, remain read-only or draft-only where specified, and preserve the same governed boundaries across multi-service compositions.

service_topology_bindings.implementation_notes:
Accepted assistant guidance:
- Formalize the capability surface each service must expose: Define the stable capability contracts each service is expected to own, including which actions are read-only, which require approval, and which must stop for clarification.
  Why: The generator and verifier need stable capability ownership, not only narrative service descriptions.
- Describe the required coordination edges between services: Capture where one service depends on another for bounded context, approvals, or downstream execution so coordination can be represented explicitly instead of as hidden glue.
  Why: Coordination edges are easier to validate when they are declared before implementation.
- Keep approval boundaries outside the main happy path: Identify which service should prepare a proposed action and which surface or role must approve it before the mutation boundary is crossed.
  Why: Approval semantics should be explicit in service design rather than buried in one implementation path.
- Preserve explanation and traceability across service boundaries: Define which service boundaries must preserve explanation, lineage, or durable evidence so later verification can reconstruct why a result or stop condition occurred.
  Why: Cross-service traceability should be deliberate, not inferred after implementation exists.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Source business specs are visible in the project, derived PM and developer artifacts trace back to them, and runtime validation references the same governed capability surface.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: An informed reviewer can see that the GTM business intent was written first and that PM-facing requirements, developer design, and running service behavior derive from that source rather than hidden prompt logic.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Representative scenario review shows supported requests resolve through declared capability surfaces, while raw export, arbitrary query, and unsupported analysis requests are denied or constrained.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: The system answers recurring GTM questions through bounded capabilities with stable inputs and governed outcomes instead of generic CRM, BI, or raw export behavior.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Representative runs across pipeline, forecast, bottleneck, team, product, enrichment, prioritization, outreach, and reassignment flows return usable governed outputs with bounded explanation and no unintended mutation.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: For in-scope requests, users receive explainable summaries, rankings, previews, or drafts that match the intended capability family and stay read-only or draft-only unless an approval-gated preview is explicitly allowed.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Runtime review of ambiguous scenarios shows clarification responses for missing required fields rather than inferred defaults.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Missing critical inputs trigger explicit clarification instead of guessing, including quarter, account scope, cohort, routing target, persona, or outreach target when required by the capability.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Comparative actor review shows allowed, masked, restricted, or denied outcomes vary appropriately by actor identity and requested scope.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Actor-aware restriction and masking remain visible in runtime behavior so that different actors can receive different allowed scope, field visibility, or outcomes for the same business question.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Preview scenarios return approval-gated outputs and do not execute writes; higher- and lower-authority actors show the expected differences in preparation versus denial.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Operational preparation flows stop cleanly at approval_required before any routing, reassignment, follow-up execution, or other downstream mutation.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Cross-service scenarios show each step uses the canonical owning capability surface, with no hidden consumer-side policy substitution or merged super-service behavior.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Service ownership and capability boundaries remain explicit across the showcase, including compound flows that span more than one service.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Representative multi-step flows demonstrate that governance survives service chaining rather than disappearing once orchestration begins.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Compound workflows preserve the same governed behaviors as single-service calls, including clarification, denial, restriction, masking, and approval stops at the correct step in the chain.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Audit review for representative calls can explain both the final outcome and why different actors received different results.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Every governed call is auditable enough to reconstruct who asked, which actor identity applied, which capability ran, which normalized parameters were used, and why the outcome was success, clarification, restriction, denial, or approval-required.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Local reproduction, Studio inspection, runtime metadata validation, and regression results all confirm the implementation still matches the intended bounded design.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: The same governed services can be reproduced locally, inspected in Studio, and validated end to end through runtime checks and regression-backed review across supported flows.

business_constraints.assistant_drafted_requirements_summary:
Define the GTM showcase as a bounded multi-service system, not a general GTM super-agent: The product must provide a governed GTM agent system composed of explicit bounded service areas for pipeline analytics, enrichment, prioritization, outreach drafting, and operational previews. It must answer recurring GTM business questions through declared capabilities rather than unconstrained CRM, warehouse, SQL, or prompt-driven exploration. The system must preserve explicit service ownership and must not collapse into a generic query-anything or do-anything surface.
Preserve the canonical capability contract across design, generation, and verification: The product must preserve the canonical public capability contract defined for the showcase so that PM requirements, developer design, generated implementations, runtime metadata, and regression validation all refer to the same governed behaviors. Accepted requirements should make clear that these capabilities are stable contract surface, remain owned by their designated service areas, and are not replaced by paraphrased identifiers in downstream artifacts.
Make actor-aware visibility, scope, and outcome differences explicit: The product must support actor-aware governance that changes what data is visible, what fields are masked, what scope is allowed, what actions can be prepared, and when requests are denied or require approval. Requirements should explicitly cover actor-sensitive outcomes such as restricted regional scope, masked financial values, and different allowed preparation behavior for different actor families.
Require explicit governed outcomes instead of silent guessing or overreach: Every bounded capability must produce one of a small set of reviewable governed outcomes: success for in-scope reads or drafts, clarification when critical inputs are missing, restriction when the actor is only partially allowed, denial for unsafe or out-of-scope requests, and approval_required for operational previews that stop before execution. The system must not silently guess missing critical fields or improvise unsupported behavior.
Require clarification for missing critical scope such as quarter, cohort, account reference, or target: The product must ask for clarification when a request omits critical scope needed for a bounded answer. This includes missing quarter for pipeline analytics and operational previews, missing account set or reference account for enrichment, missing cohort or routing target for prioritization, and missing target, persona, channel, or objective for outreach drafting. The system must not infer these fields when the business specs require explicit scope.
Support bounded pipeline analytics views with explainable summaries and contributors: The product must support bounded pipeline analytics capabilities that answer recurring GTM review questions for a quarter and optional allowed scope. Accepted requirements should cover forecast summaries with declared forecast modes, stage bottleneck summaries with one bounded slice dimension, sales team performance summaries with one bounded slice dimension, and product pipeline summaries with optional product focus. These views must return explainable rollups, evidence, or top contributors rather than raw row-level exports.
Support bounded account enrichment and lookalike analysis without becoming a raw enrichment surface: The product must let users request bounded enrichment summaries for a selected account set, explain why an account is a strong or weak fit, and identify lookalike accounts using bounded similarity logic. It must stay within explicit account or segment scope, remain read-only in this phase, and deny raw bulk enrichment exports or requests that drift into outreach, scoring, or operational mutation.
Support governed prioritization with explainable ranking and routing posture recommendations: The product must score a bounded cohort of leads or accounts, rank the highest-priority items with bounded rationale, and recommend routing posture such as sales, nurture, or archive. It must deny requests for raw model-feature or training-detail exports, must not silently route or mutate downstream systems, and must stop at approval_required when a request moves from recommendation into routing execution.
Keep outreach bounded to draft preparation and variants, not send execution: The product must draft bounded outreach content for an explicit lead or account target, support follow-up and objection-response variants, and tailor drafts using explicit context. It must ask for clarification when target or message intent is unclear, deny send requests and raw transcript or training-corpus export requests, and remain draft-only in the first cut without mutating CRM engagement state.
Separate operational preparation from execution with a hard approval boundary: The product must support bounded operational previews such as reassignment planning, follow-up preparation, and routing preparation, but it must stop before any downstream write or ownership change. Requirements should state that preview generation is allowed only within actor authority, that the next step returns approval_required, and that direct execution requests are denied or stopped rather than carried out automatically.
Support governed cross-service composition without hiding policy in app glue: The product must support compound GTM questions that span more than one bounded service hop, such as pipeline or risk to enrichment, prioritization to enrichment to outreach, forecast to risk to follow-up preview, score to route, and bottleneck to risk to enrichment. These flows must preserve service-owned capability boundaries, actor-aware restrictions, clarification behavior, denial behavior, and approval stops at the correct step rather than relying on hidden consumer-side logic.
Require auditability for every governed call and actor-differentiated result: The product must make every governed call reconstructable after the fact. Requirements should state that the system can explain who asked, which actor identity applied, which capability ran, which parameters were used after normalization, what governed outcome occurred, and why different actors may have received different results. Auditability must be treated as part of the business trust story, not only as an implementation detail.
Make the full business-to-runtime validation path a product requirement: The product must make the full lifecycle visible and reviewable: source business specs as first-class artifacts, PM-readable requirements derived from those specs, developer design derived from accepted requirements, generated code used in the running implementation, explicit extension points outside overwritten generated files, runtime metadata that Studio can inspect, and regression-backed validation across single-service and compound flows. Requirements should also distinguish what the BI verification surface can validate versus what remains unique to governed ANIP runtime behavior.

context.actor_context: sales_leader or rev_ops_manager reviewing quarter forecast posture
context.business_scope: Bounded forecast review inside the GTM pipeline service with optional actor-visible regional scope
context.rationale: Directly supported by the forecast business spec and umbrella showcase spec, including quarter scoping, declared forecast modes, explainability, and governed read-only behavior.

expected_anip_support: Expose a stable forecast capability with declared input metadata for quarter, forecast mode, and optional scope.
Execute aggregate forecast reads through the semantic layer.
Return governed outcomes that can show success, clarification, restriction, or masking.
Preserve auditability of actor, normalized parameters, and outcome.
expected_behavior: Require a declared quarter rather than guessing one.
Support declared forecast modes such as risk_adjusted, likely, and best_case only.
Allow optional regional or owner-visible scoping when permitted for the actor.
Return explainable stage rollups and top contributors.
Do not expose raw row-level CRM exports.
Do not mutate downstream systems.
scenario.name: bounded-quarterly-pipeline-forecast-review
scenario.narrative: A GTM leader asks for a risk-adjusted, likely, or best-case pipeline forecast for a specific quarter. The system returns bounded forecast totals, explainable stage rollups, and top contributing open opportunities or accounts for the actor-visible scope.

scenarios.title: Review a bounded quarterly pipeline forecast with explainable rollups

context.actor_context: Any actor otherwise allowed to request forecast summaries
context.business_scope: Required input completion for bounded forecast behavior
context.rationale: Explicitly called out in the forecast spec as a required clarification behavior and repeated in the umbrella governance rules.
context.time_scope: Quarter missing and must be supplied

expected_anip_support: Represent clarification as an explicit governed outcome.
Expose required input metadata so missing quarter can be detected consistently.
Log the clarification stop for audit and regression review.
expected_behavior: Do not infer the quarter from current date or prior conversation alone.
Ask for the minimum missing field needed to continue.
Resume bounded forecast behavior only after the quarter is provided.
scenario.name: forecast-clarification-when-quarter-is-missing
scenario.narrative: A GTM user asks for a pipeline forecast but omits the quarter. The system stops and requests the missing quarter instead of inferring one from context.

scenarios.title: Clarify missing quarter before running a forecast

context.actor_context: Comparison across sales_leader and account_manager_east or account_manager_west
context.business_scope: Actor-aware visibility, scope restriction, and masking for forecast review
context.rationale: Forecast spec explicitly requires actor-aware restriction and masking, and the umbrella spec requires different governed outcomes by actor identity.

expected_anip_support: Return explicit restricted and masked outcomes in the runtime contract.
Preserve actor-aware policy evaluation as reviewable behavior, not hidden prompt logic.
Record why two actors received different results.
expected_behavior: Actor identity changes what scope is visible.
Requests beyond allowed region or ownership boundary return restricted behavior.
Some actors may see forecast shape but not full financial values.
The system must not bypass restriction or masking rules.
scenario.name: forecast-actor-aware-restriction-and-masking
scenario.narrative: A sales leader and a region-scoped account manager ask similar forecast questions. The sales leader can receive the bounded forecast for the requested scope, while the narrower actor is restricted from disallowed regions or receives masked financial values when only partial visibility is allowed.

scenarios.title: Apply actor-aware restriction or masking on forecast visibility

context.actor_context: sales_leader or rev_ops_manager reviewing pipeline friction
context.business_scope: Bounded stage bottleneck review inside the GTM pipeline service
context.rationale: Directly supported by the stage bottleneck spec, including quarter requirement, one bounded slice dimension, explainable evidence, and actor-aware governance.

expected_anip_support: Expose declared slice inputs and bounded output shape.
Execute aggregate bottleneck reads through the semantic layer.
Support explicit clarification, restriction, masking, and denial outcomes.
expected_behavior: Require a quarter rather than guessing.
Support one bounded slice dimension at a time.
Return top bottleneck rows with explainable evidence.
Restrict or mask results based on actor visibility.
Deny raw exports or unsupported slicing.
scenario.name: bounded-stage-bottleneck-review
scenario.narrative: A user asks where open pipeline is getting stuck in a quarter. The system returns a bounded bottleneck summary, optionally scoped to an allowed region and sliced by one declared dimension, with evidence about stage accumulation, aging, and risk.

scenarios.title: Review stage bottlenecks for a quarter with one declared slice

context.actor_context: sales_leader or rev_ops_manager reviewing team performance
context.business_scope: Bounded sales team performance review inside the GTM pipeline service
context.rationale: Directly grounded in the sales team performance spec and aligned with the umbrella business questions around team and manager performance.

expected_anip_support: Expose stable inputs for quarter, optional region, and one slice dimension.
Return governed read outcomes without raw row-level exports.
Preserve auditability of actor and normalized scope.
expected_behavior: Summarize team or manager performance for a quarter.
Support one bounded slice dimension at a time.
Return explainable performance rows with pipeline and risk context.
Clarify missing quarter instead of guessing.
Restrict or mask results when actor visibility is narrower.
scenario.name: bounded-sales-team-performance-review
scenario.narrative: A GTM leader asks how teams or managers are performing in a quarter. The system returns a bounded team-performance summary with explainable evidence about open pipeline, wins, and stall pressure, optionally scoped to an allowed region and one declared slice.

scenarios.title: Review sales team performance for a quarter by one allowed slice

context.actor_context: sales_leader, rev_ops_manager, or product-facing leader reviewing product posture
context.business_scope: Bounded product pipeline review inside the GTM pipeline service
context.rationale: Directly supported by the product pipeline spec, including quarter scoping, optional product focus, explainable product rows, and actor-aware governance.

expected_anip_support: Expose stable inputs for quarter, optional region, and optional product.
Return explainable product rows through a governed read contract.
Support explicit restriction and masking outcomes.
expected_behavior: Summarize product pipeline for a quarter.
Optionally narrow to one declared product.
Restrict actor scope when a user asks for a region they should not see.
Mask financial values for actors who should not receive full numbers.
Deny raw exports.
scenario.name: bounded-product-pipeline-review
scenario.narrative: A user asks how products are performing in a quarter or requests a named product view. The system returns a bounded product summary with open, won, lost, and risk context for the allowed scope.

scenarios.title: Review product pipeline posture for a quarter or named product

context.actor_context: Authorized sales_leader or rev_ops_manager preparing operational changes
context.business_scope: Approval-gated reassignment planning inside the GTM pipeline service
context.rationale: Directly supported by the reassignment preview spec and umbrella approval posture. This is a core operational-preview scenario with explicit stop conditions.

expected_anip_support: Represent approval_required as a first-class governed outcome.
Preserve preview-versus-execution separation in the contract.
Record actor, scope, basis, and approval stop for audit.
expected_behavior: Prepare a bounded reassignment preview for a quarter.
Optionally narrow to one allowed region.
Support one declared reassignment basis at a time.
Return explainable source and target manager recommendations.
Return approval_required instead of mutating assignments.
Deny direct execution requests.
scenario.name: reassignment-preview-with-approval-stop
scenario.narrative: A GTM leader or RevOps operator asks for a reassignment plan for overloaded coverage in a quarter. The system prepares a bounded preview of recommended source and target managers, explains the basis for the recommendation, and stops at approval_required before any ownership mutation.

scenarios.title: Prepare a reassignment preview but stop at approval

context.actor_context: Revenue operator or GTM user reviewing selected accounts
context.business_scope: Bounded account enrichment over an explicit account set
context.rationale: Directly supported by the enrichment spec and umbrella composition requirements, including bounded account scope, explainable evidence, and non-mutating behavior.
context.time_scope: No fixed quarter required unless the account set comes from a quarter-based upstream review

expected_anip_support: Expose stable inputs for selected accounts or bounded segment scope.
Return explainable enrichment evidence without raw source dumps.
Support clarification when account scope is missing.
expected_behavior: Require an explicit account set rather than guessing one.
Summarize firmographic context and fit evidence.
Stay within explicit account or segment scope.
Deny raw bulk enrichment export requests.
Remain read-only.
scenario.name: bounded-account-enrichment-summary
scenario.narrative: A GTM user asks for enrichment context for a selected set of accounts. The system returns bounded firmographic and fit summaries that explain why the accounts matter, without exposing raw enrichment datasets or mutating downstream systems.

scenarios.title: Summarize bounded enrichment context for selected accounts

context.actor_context: sales_leader or rev_ops_manager preparing routing decisions
context.business_scope: Approval-gated routing preparation in the prioritization service
context.rationale: Explicitly supported by the prioritization spec and umbrella approval posture. This is a key operational boundary for the prioritization service.
context.time_scope: Bounded cohort or source window must be explicit

expected_anip_support: Represent approval_required explicitly in the contract.
Preserve separation between recommendation and execution.
Record actor, cohort, routing target, and approval stop for audit.
expected_behavior: Do not silently route or mutate downstream systems.
Return a reviewable routing recommendation or preview.
Require approval before route execution.
Deny requests for raw scoring internals.
scenario.name: prioritized-routing-preview-with-approval-stop
scenario.narrative: A user asks to route prioritized leads downstream. The system prepares a bounded routing preview and returns approval_required before any routing or CRM mutation occurs.

scenarios.title: Prepare lead routing but stop before execution

context.actor_context: GTM user or RevOps operator prioritizing work
context.business_scope: Bounded scoring, ranking, and routing recommendation
context.rationale: Directly supported by the prioritization spec, including bounded cohort scoring, explainable hot/warm/cold rationale, and recommendation without mutation.
context.time_scope: May use a bounded source window such as last week when explicitly provided

expected_anip_support: Expose explicit prioritization capability inputs for cohort and optional routing target.
Return explainable ranking outputs without leaking backend scoring internals.
Support approval-gated follow-on routing preparation.
expected_behavior: Require a bounded cohort or source window.
Explain why an item is hot, warm, or cold using bounded evidence.
Recommend routing posture without mutating downstream systems.
Deny raw model-feature or training-data export requests.
Ask for clarification when cohort or routing target is unclear.
scenario.name: bounded-prioritization-and-routing-recommendation
scenario.narrative: A GTM user asks which leads or accounts should be worked first. The system scores a bounded cohort, ranks the top items, explains why they are hot, warm, or cold, and recommends a safe routing posture without mutating downstream systems.

scenarios.title: Score and rank a bounded cohort with routing recommendations

context.actor_context: Revenue operator or GTM user exploring similar accounts
context.business_scope: Bounded similarity analysis in the enrichment service
context.rationale: Explicitly supported by the enrichment spec as a main behavior class with bounded similarity analysis.
context.time_scope: Not quarter-bound unless the user narrows the source cohort upstream

expected_anip_support: Expose a stable reference-account input.
Return bounded similarity results with rationale.
Support clarification when the reference account is missing.
expected_behavior: Require a reference account when similarity is requested.
Use bounded similarity logic.
Return explainable matching evidence.
Deny unsupported raw enrichment exports or unrelated outreach/scoring requests.
scenario.name: bounded-lookalike-account-analysis
scenario.narrative: A user asks for accounts similar to a named reference account. The system returns a bounded set of explainable lookalike accounts using approved similarity logic and does not expose raw enrichment internals.

scenarios.title: Find bounded lookalike accounts from a named reference account

context.actor_context: sales_leader or rev_ops_manager preparing outreach content
context.business_scope: Draft-only outreach generation for an explicit target
context.rationale: Directly supported by the outreach spec, including draft-only behavior, explicit target context, clarification needs, and denial of send requests.
context.time_scope: Not inherently quarter-bound; may inherit context from upstream review

expected_anip_support: Expose stable inputs for target, persona, channel, and message goal.
Return bounded draft outputs and variants without send behavior.
Support explicit denial for transcript dumps or direct-send asks.
expected_behavior: Require explicit target and enough context to draft safely.
Allow follow-up or objection-response variants when requested.
Ask for clarification when target, persona, channel, or objective is unclear.
Deny send requests, bulk scraping requests, and raw transcript export requests.
Keep output draft-only.
scenario.name: bounded-outreach-draft-generation
scenario.narrative: A GTM user asks for outreach content for a selected account or lead. The system drafts a bounded message and optional variants using explicit target context, while stopping short of any outbound send action.

scenarios.title: Draft outreach for a selected target without sending

context.actor_context: GTM user or revenue operator following up on at-risk accounts
context.business_scope: Cross-service composition from pipeline review into enrichment over a bounded account set
context.rationale: The umbrella spec explicitly names pipeline/risk to enrichment composition, but the detailed at-risk pipeline source text is only partially present in the provided brief. The composition pattern is supported, while exact upstream capability details are less explicit here.
context.time_scope: Often quarter-bound in the upstream pipeline step

expected_anip_support: Support explicit service-owned capability IDs across the chain.
Preserve governed boundaries across multi-service execution.
Make each step auditable with actor, parameters, and outcome.
expected_behavior: Keep the account set bounded across the service chain.
Do not expose raw enrichment data.
Preserve clarification, denial, and actor-aware boundaries at each step.
Do not turn the composition into generic cross-system exploration.
scenario.name: at-risk-pipeline-review-to-account-enrichment
scenario.narrative: A GTM user first reviews a bounded set of at-risk accounts from pipeline analysis, then requests enrichment context for those same accounts. The system chains the governed services without expanding scope or exposing raw data.

scenarios.title: Move from at-risk pipeline review into bounded account enrichment

context.actor_context: Reviewer, operator, or PM validating governed runtime behavior
context.business_scope: Auditability and explanation across single-service and multi-service flows
context.capability: ask user to confirm which runtime or review surface should own this scenario
context.rationale: Strongly supported by the umbrella spec's auditability requirements and success criteria. This is a cross-cutting PM scenario needed for trust and validation.
context.time_scope: Applies after any governed call or composed workflow

expected_anip_support: Expose enough runtime metadata and validation surface to compare intended and observed behavior.
Preserve auditability across clarification, restriction, denial, masking, success, and approval-required outcomes.
Support review across single-service and compound flows.
expected_behavior: A reviewer can reconstruct actor, capability, normalized parameters, and governed outcome.
The system can explain why two actors received different results.
Audit is part of the trust story, not an internal-only concern.
scenario.name: audit-explanation-for-governed-outcome-differences
scenario.narrative: A reviewer examines a governed call after the fact and asks why the system completed, clarified, restricted, denied, masked, or stopped for approval. The system can reconstruct who asked, which actor identity applied, which capability ran, which normalized parameters were used, and why the outcome differed across actors.

scenarios.title: Explain why two actors received different governed outcomes

context.actor_context: GTM user moving from prioritization into outreach preparation
context.business_scope: Cross-service composition from prioritization into draft-only outreach
context.rationale: The umbrella spec explicitly names prioritization to outreach composition and defines a dedicated prioritized outreach draft capability owned by the outreach service.
context.time_scope: Bounded cohort or source window must be explicit in the prioritization step

expected_anip_support: Support service-owned cross-service capability semantics.
Preserve bounded target selection in the public contract.
Make the composed flow auditable across both services.
expected_behavior: Keep the target explicit and bounded between services.
Do not silently route leads or send messages.
Do not mix enrichment or scoring behavior into the outreach step.
Preserve clarification and denial behavior at the correct step.
scenario.name: prioritized-target-to-outreach-draft
scenario.narrative: A GTM user first ranks a bounded cohort to identify the highest-priority target, then requests outreach drafting for that explicit prioritized target. The system composes the flow across services while preserving bounded target selection and draft-only behavior.

scenarios.title: Draft outreach for a prioritized target without leaking into routing or send behavior
